TTE signs of PH: decreased TAPSE (<18mm), decreased S′ (<9.5 cm/s), enlarged right atrial area (>18 cm2), increased peak TRV.
Table 6 Group 3 PH associated with lung diseases/hypoxia: 3.1 obstructive lung disease or emphysema.

kg_retrieval.chunk: Echo of PH: dilated RV with basal RV/LV ratio > 1.0, flattened septum (D-shaped LV), distended IVC. RVOT AT < 105 ms with notch indicates pre-capillary PH; RV-FAC reduced.
CT signs of PH: enlarged PA diameter, PA/AO ratio > 0.9, enlarged right heart. Combination PA ≥ 30 mm + RVOT wall ≥ 6 mm + septal deviation ≥ 140° (or RV:LV ≥ 1) highly predictive of PH.
Composite echo score identifies severe PH using RA area, RV:LV ratio, LVEI. PA enlargement, RV outflow hypertrophy, increased RV:LV ratio suggest PH in lung disease.
Group 3 PH associated with lung diseases/hypoxia: 3.1 obstructive lung disease or emphysema; 3.2 restrictive; 3.4 hypoventilation syndromes.
Group 3 PH: elderly males, smoking common; oxygen requirement common; PFT abnormal; DLCO often very low (< 45%); CT parenchymal lung disease; RHC pre-capillary PH.

kg_retrieval.chunk: Echo parameters of PH: enlarged RV, dilated RV with basal RV/LV ratio > 1.0 (four-chamber view), flattened septum 'D-shaped' LV, distended IVC; RVOT AT < 105 ms with mid-systolic notch indicates pre-capillary PH; RV-FAC reduced (< 35%).

CT signs of PH: enlarged PA diameter, PA-to-aorta ratio > 0.9, enlarged right heart. Combination of PA ≥ 30 mm, RVOT wall ≥ 6 mm, and septal deviation ≥ 140° (or RV:LV ≥ 1) is highly predictive of PH. Non-contrast CT helps determine cause when parenchymal lung disease present.

A composite echo score can identify severe PH using RA area, RV:LV ratio, LV eccentricity index. Combining echo with contrast-enhanced CT aids classification; PA enlargement, RV outflow hypertrophy, and increased RV:LV ratio may suggest PH.

Pathophysiology of group 3 PH involves remodelling of pulmonary vessels including vascular pruning, plus airway/parenchyma remodelling. As vascular remodelling and hypoxaemia increase, PH progresses no PH → non-severe → severe (PVR > 5 WU).

Group 3 PH associated with lung diseases and/or hypoxia: 3.1 obstructive lung disease or emphysema; 3.2 restrictive; 3.3 mixed; 3.4 hypoventilation; 3.5 hypoxia without lung disease; 3.6 developmental.
